metadata.title: Argentina Corruption Scandals 2024-2026
metadata.description: Research compilation of major political-financial-judicial corruption scandals in Argentina under the Milei administration and related legacy cases
metadata.source_methodology: Web research aggregation from major Argentine and international media outlets
metadata.disclaimer: All allegations are as reported by cited sources. Judicial outcomes noted where available.

scandals.name: Caso $LIBRA / Crypto Memecoin Scandal
scandals.summary: President Milei promoted the $LIBRA memecoin on X (formerly Twitter) on Feb 14, 2025. The token surged to $4B market cap then collapsed 90%+, with insiders cashing out $107M. ~44,000 investors lost money, with total losses estimated at $100M+. A $5M payment agreement between Milei and the token creators was found on a lobbyist's phone.

key_persons.name: Javier Milei
key_persons.involvement: Published promotional post for $LIBRA on X; alleged $5M payment agreement found; exchanged calls with promoters minutes before launch

key_persons.name: Hayden Mark Davis
key_persons.involvement: Created the $LIBRA token; met Milei at Casa Rosada in 2024; assets frozen by Argentine judge
key_persons.status: Assets frozen; filed declaration in New York denying scam

key_persons.name: Mauricio Novelli
key_persons.role: Crypto trader, lobbyist, operator of N&W Professional Traders
key_persons.involvement: Intermediary between $LIBRA promoters and Milei's inner circle; $5M payment note found on his phone; had multi-year financial relationship with Milei dating to 2021; exchanged 5+ calls with Milei minutes before the promotional post

key_persons.name: Karina Milei
key_persons.role: Secretary General of the Presidency, sister of President
key_persons.involvement: Phone records show she was in communication loop during token launch and collapse

key_persons.name: Santiago Caputo
key_persons.involvement: Phone records show frantic communication with Novelli during the token launch

key_persons.name: Gideon Davis
key_persons.role: Brother of Hayden Davis, Kelsier Ventures
key_persons.involvement: Part of Kelsier Ventures family operation

key_persons.name: Tom Davis
key_persons.role: Father of Hayden Davis, Kelsier Ventures
key_persons.involvement: Background includes counterfeiting allegations; family has 'chequered past' per Buenos Aires Times

entities.name: Kelsier Ventures
entities.role: Created and launched the $LIBRA token

entities.name: N&W Professional Traders
entities.role: Novelli's trading firm; paid Milei for crypto education classes since 2021

financial_impact.alleged_payment_to_milei: $5 million (three-tier structure: $1.5M advance, $1.5M for public endorsement, $2M for consulting contract)

judicial_status.congressional_commission: Reactivated March 2026; Milei expected to give explanations before Congress
judicial_status.asset_freezes: Hayden Davis assets frozen by Argentine judge
judicial_status.government_position: Claims no record of contract between Milei and Hayden Davis

scandals.name: BCRA Gold Reserves Sent Abroad / Lack of Transparency
scandals.summary: In mid-2024, the BCRA secretly transferred approximately 37 tonnes of gold (60% of total metal reserves, ~$1B) to London via four flights from Ezeiza airport. The BCRA refuses to disclose details citing 'security', claims no contracts exist for the placement, and has blocked AGN audits. Courts have ordered disclosure but the BCRA continues to resist.

key_persons.name: Santiago Bausili
key_persons.involvement: Oversaw the gold transfer; blocked AGN audit access

key_persons.name: Luis Caputo
key_persons.involvement: Confirmed gold was sent to London; stated it would not be the only such operation

key_persons.name: Juan Manuel Olmos
key_persons.role: President of the AGN (National Audit Office)
key_persons.involvement: Summoned BCRA to report on gold destination; BCRA refused cooperation

entities.name: Banco Central de la Republica Argentina (BCRA)
entities.role: Transferred gold reserves abroad without transparency

entities.name: Auditoria General de la Nacion (AGN)
entities.role: Attempted audit blocked by BCRA

entities.name: Bank for International Settlements (BIS)
entities.role: Possible destination; BCRA claims no contracts exist with BIS or other foreign depositaries

financial_impact.value: ~$1 billion at time of transfer (now higher due to gold rally)
financial_impact.gold_valuation_gain: $4.5 billion gain in BCRA gold holdings due to price rally under Milei

judicial_status.agn_audit: Blocked by BCRA; AGN filling vacancies to advance investigation
judicial_status.court_orders: Camara Contencioso Administrativo Federal ordered BCRA to disclose information, reversing lower court's backing of total secrecy
judicial_status.bcra_position: Claims contracts 'do not exist'; cites security for withholding details
judicial_status.status: Ongoing - BCRA continues blocking audit as of March 2026

scandals.name: Aerolineas Argentinas Privatization Push
scandals.summary: The Milei government declared Aerolineas Argentinas 'subject to privatization' by decree, seeking to sell 51% of shares to international groups (Latam, Avianca/Abra, Air France-KLM). The process was paused before legislative changeover in late 2025 and is expected to be re-introduced in Congress in early 2026. The airline achieved its first positive operating result in 2024 after years of ~$400M annual losses.

key_persons.name: Javier Milei
key_persons.involvement: Signed decree declaring airline subject to privatization; previously threatened closure as alternative

entities.name: Aerolineas Argentinas
entities.role: Target of privatization

entities.name: Latam Airlines
entities.role: Potential buyer being sounded out

entities.name: Avianca / Abra Group
entities.role: Potential buyer being sounded out

entities.name: Air France-KLM
entities.role: Potential buyer being sounded out

judicial_status.status: Legislative process; decree issued but congressional approval pending for H1 2026

scandals.name: SIDE Scandal / Santiago Caputo Shadow Power Network
scandals.summary: Presidential adviser Santiago Caputo controls a vast parallel power network through the SIDE (intelligence agency), which received 100 billion pesos in reserved (unaudited) funds. A SIDE contract was awarded to Tactic COC LLC, owned by Leonardo Scatturice (a Caputo associate with intelligence background), who then acquired airline Flybondi with unclear funding. Caputo also controls appointments across Health, ENARSA, UIF, Justice Ministry, and YPF. A DNU (941/2025) expanded SIDE powers to include detention without judicial order. The 'Iron Triangle' (Milei-Karina Milei-Caputo) is fracturing as Karina seeks to take over Caputo's domains.

key_persons.name: Santiago Caputo
key_persons.role: Presidential adviser ('monje negro'); nephew of Economy Minister Luis Caputo
key_persons.involvement: Controls SIDE appointments, ENARSA, Health, UIF, Justice Ministry designations; linked to Scatturice contracts; former Move Group consultant; has final say before presidential decisions per Milei himself

key_persons.name: Sergio Neiffert
key_persons.involvement: Appointed by Caputo; denied Caputo controls SIDE; son Lautaro linked to shady security business deals

key_persons.name: Lautaro Neiffert
key_persons.involvement: Allegedly involved in business dealings with security companies using SIDE resources

key_persons.name: Leonardo Scatturice
key_persons.involvement: Received SIDE contract via Tactic COC LLC; subsequently acquired Flybondi airline; registered as SIDE lobbyist with US DOJ; dubbed 'Lazaro Baez libertario'

key_persons.name: Lourdes Arrieta
key_persons.involvement: Filed complaint to Bicameral Intelligence Commission exposing the network

key_persons.name: Karina Milei
key_persons.involvement: Moving to take over Caputo's areas of influence including SIDE and ARCA in 2026; expanding power over 4 of 9 ministries

key_persons.name: Guillermo Garat
key_persons.role: Former Caputo associate from Move Group consultancy
key_persons.involvement: Appointed to YPF Institutional Relations

key_persons.name: Andres Vazquez
key_persons.involvement: Identified as close to Caputo; potential target of Karina's power moves

judicial_status.status: Active investigations in Congress and judiciary

scandals.name: YPF Privatization & Vaca Muerta Deals / Board Patronage
scandals.summary: While full YPF privatization is blocked (requires 2/3 congressional supermajority), the government has packed the YPF board with political allies. Former Cabinet Chief Francos and former Interior Minister Catalan were given board seats (Catalan's paying 140M pesos/year). Manuel Adorni was made board president in Jan 2026. Santiago Caputo's former Move Group associate Guillermo Garat was placed in YPF Institutional Relations. The Rocca family (Techint) shares control of YPF with the government. Major LNG export project (~$40B total investment) underway with ENI.

key_persons.name: Horacio Marin
key_persons.involvement: Leading company operations and Vaca Muerta expansion

key_persons.name: Guillermo Francos
key_persons.role: Former Chief of Cabinet, YPF Board member
key_persons.involvement: Moved from board president to alternate seat when replaced by Adorni

key_persons.role: Former Interior Minister, YPF Board Titular Director Class D
key_persons.involvement: Appointed Nov 2025; salary ~140M pesos; criticized as 'Casta Libertaria'

key_persons.name: Manuel Adorni
key_persons.role: Chief of Cabinet (current), YPF Board President
key_persons.involvement: Designated Jan 2026 as State representative with Golden Share

key_persons.name: Guillermo Garat
key_persons.role: Former Santiago Caputo / Move Group associate
key_persons.involvement: Placed at YPF Institutional Relations

key_persons.name: Paolo Rocca
key_persons.involvement: Shares control of YPF with Milei government

judicial_status.privatization_legal_status: Blocked; removed from Ley Bases; requires 2/3 Congress + provincial approval
judicial_status.status: No active judicial proceedings on board appointments

scandals.name: Causa Cuadernos (Bribery Notebooks Trial)
scandals.summary: Argentina's largest corruption case: trial of 87 defendants (including ex-President Cristina Fernandez de Kirchner, 19 ex-officials, 65 businessmen, 2 drivers) began Nov 6, 2025. Based on notebooks of ex-driver Oscar Centeno documenting systematic bribery for public works contracts. 36 accused businessmen attempted to pay for dismissal under 'reparacion integral'. Defense motions to invalidate the notebooks and the cooperating witness law were rejected by the tribunal in March 2026.

key_persons.name: Cristina Fernandez de Kirchner
key_persons.involvement: Principal accused; indagatoria (formal questioning) scheduled

key_persons.name: Oscar Centeno

judicial_status.defense_motions: Rejected by tribunal March 2026 - trial continues
judicial_status.key_legal_issues: Validity of notebooks, constitutionality of cooperating witness law (Ley 27.304), UIF standing

scandals.name: Ministerio de Capital Humano / Retained Food & Irregular Contracts
scandals.summary: In May 2024, it was revealed that the Ministry of Capital Humano retained ~5,000 tonnes of food in warehouses while cutting deliveries to community dining halls amid rising poverty. Minister Pettovello fired Secretary Pablo de la Torre and accused him of running an illegal political fundraising scheme through OEI contracts. However, Pettovello herself cannot account for 8.3 billion pesos (more than half of 14 billion authorized for food purchases). The ministry was raided by judicial order.

key_persons.name: Sandra Pettovello
key_persons.involvement: Oversaw retention of food; cannot account for 8.3B pesos; fired De la Torre; blocked public information access on contracts

key_persons.name: Pablo de la Torre

scandals.name: Systematic DNU (Emergency Decree) Abuse
scandals.summary: In under two years, Milei signed 83 DNUs, surpassing Cristina Kirchner's 81 in eight years. In 2025 alone, 35 DNUs were issued vs. only 13 laws enacted by Congress. During the Dec 2025-Feb 2026 legislative recess, 11 DNUs were issued (equaling all laws from 2025). Multiple DNUs declared unconstitutional by courts, including on gender identity, anti-strike measures, Supreme Court appointments, and seizure of corruption-related assets. DNU 941/2025 gave SIDE power to detain without judicial order.

key_persons.name: Javier Milei
key_persons.involvement: Signed all 83+ DNUs

dnus_declared_unconstitutional.subject: Intelligence reform giving SIDE detention powers without judicial order
dnus_declared_unconstitutional.status: Multiple amparos filed; one rejected at first instance

financial_impact.note: DNUs bypass congressional oversight on budgetary and regulatory matters

judicial_status.status: Pattern of executive overreach; courts pushing back case by case

scandals.name: Causa Chocolate / Buenos Aires Legislature Ghost Employees
scandals.summary: Political operative 'Chocolate' Rigau was caught in Sept 2023 withdrawing cash from 48 debit cards belonging to phantom employees of the Buenos Aires provincial legislature. Claudio Albini (subdirector of Personnel) ran an illicit association that registered ghost workers whose salaries funded PJ political operations. Damage exceeded 800M pesos. All major parties implicated. Case elevated to oral trial in May 2025; Albini family under house arrest.

key_persons.involvement: Head of the illicit association; detained

key_persons.involvement: Part of the scheme; detained, later house arrest

financial_impact.scheme: Ghost employees registered across legislature; salaries withdrawn and diverted to political operations

scandals.name: Macri Family Judicial Developments
scandals.summary: Multiple judicial fronts for the Macri family: (1) Correo Argentino - Supreme Court reactivated the case in June 2025 after defense appeals were rejected; the case alleges an agreement during Macri's presidency that would have forgiven 98.87% of Correo Argentino's debt to the state. (2) Jorge Macri money laundering - Supreme Court revoked his acquittal in Feb 2026, reopening investigation of alleged money laundering in Miami. (3) 'Mesa Judicial' - Macri was acquitted of charges of pressuring judges (confirmed May 2024). (4) Mauricio Macri called to testify in 'Suenos Compartidos' case (March 2026).

key_persons.role: Former President (2015-2019), current PRO party president
key_persons.involvement: Acquitted in Mesa Judicial case; Correo Argentino case reactivated; testifying in Suenos Compartidos

iron_triangle.description: The 'Triangulo de Hierro' (Iron Triangle) is the informal power structure at the core of the Milei government
members: Karina Milei (Secretary General - expanding power, controls Defense, Security, Justice, Interior)
Santiago Caputo (Adviser - controls SIDE, Health, ENARSA, UIF, ARCA; influence waning in 2026)
iron_triangle.current_dynamics: As of March 2026, the triangle is fracturing. Karina is moving to take over Caputo's domains (SIDE, ARCA). Insiders describe an 'inverted triangle' with Karina dominant. The $LIBRA scandal has further weakened Caputo's position.
iron_triangle.financial_nexus: Luis 'Toto' Caputo (Economy Minister) is Santiago Caputo's uncle, creating a family link between political influence operations and economic policy

recurring_patterns: Opacity and resistance to audit/transparency (BCRA gold, SIDE funds, Capital Humano contracts)
Use of DNUs to bypass congressional oversight (83+ in <2 years, multiple struck down)
Political patronage in state companies (YPF board packed with ex-ministers)
International intermediaries in opaque deals (Hayden Davis/Kelsier, Scatturice/Tactic COC)
Intelligence apparatus used for political/financial operations (SIDE reserved funds to Caputo associates)

connections_to_existing_investigations.note: The following connections to the Epstein investigation framework are structural rather than direct
patterns: Offshore financial structures (Kelsier Ventures in Delaware; Scatturice's Miami operations; Jorge Macri Miami laundering)
Intelligence agency misuse for political protection (SIDE parallels to intelligence community role in Epstein case)
Judicial capture and selective prosecution (Mesa Judicial acquittal; Lijo's inaction on Correo Argentino; Lijo then nominated to Supreme Court by Milei)
Financial elite impunity mechanisms (36 Cuadernos defendants attempting to buy their way out of trial)
